scenarios.title: Flat rate margin calculation with higher short market value
scenarios.intent: Verify flat rate margin is correctly calculated when short market value exceeds long market value for a sub-margin group
given: Positions are identified per §3.2.2 with sub-margin groups defined on HKEX website
Sub-group 1 contains long positions with total absolute market value of 30,000,000 HKD
Sub-group 1 contains short positions with total absolute market value of 60,000,000 HKD
when: Flat rate margin calculation is performed for sub-group 1
then: All short sub-group 1 positions are included in the flat rate margin calculation
All long sub-group 1 positions are excluded from the flat rate margin calculation
The product of absolute position market value and flat margin rate is summed
Flat rate margin multiplier is applied to obtain final flat rate margin
assumptions: Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are available
Margin multiplier is obtained from Daily Participant Margin Multiplier Report (DWH0081C)

evidence.quote: the total absolute short market value is higher than the total absolute long market value
all short sub-group 1 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation while all long sub-group 1 positions will be excluded
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin with zero market value on one side
scenarios.intent: Verify flat rate margin calculation handles sub-margin groups where one side has zero total market value
given: Sub-group 2 contains long positions with total absolute market value of 750,000 HKD
Sub-group 2 contains short positions with total absolute market value of 0 HKD
when: Flat rate margin calculation compares long and short market values for sub-group 2
then: All long sub-group 2 positions are included in the flat rate margin calculation
Short positions with zero market value are excluded
assumptions: Zero market value represents the boundary condition for position inclusion

evidence.quote: take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them
all long sub-group 2 positions and all short sub-group 3 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin calculation validates required position data
scenarios.intent: Verify that flat rate margin calculation validates presence of required position identification and market value data
given: Position data is submitted for flat rate margin calculation
Positions are identified per instructions in §3.2.2
when: Flat rate margin calculation is performed
then: Each position has valid InstrumentID
Each position has valid Quantity with sign indicating long (>=0) or short (<0)
Each position has valid absolute market value in HKD equivalent
Positions are correctly assigned to sub-margin groups defined on HKEX website
assumptions: Market value conversion to HKD equivalent is performed prior to flat rate margin calculation

evidence.quote: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market value of short positions separately for each position identified as per instructions in §3.2.2
compare the total absolute market value of long positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group of flat rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin rounding with standard rounding parameter
scenarios.intent: Verify aggregated market-risk-component margin is correctly calculated and rounded up to the nearest rounding parameter
given: Portfolio margin is 10,000,000
Flat rate margin is 15,180,000
Liquidation risk add-on is 266,865
Structured product add-on is 550,000
Corporate action position margin is 2,500,000
Holiday add-on is 18,433,039
Rounding parameter from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: Aggregated market-risk-component margin is calculated and rounded
then: Aggregated margin before rounding equals 46,929,904
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,930,000
assumptions: All margin components are provided in HKD equivalent
Rounding parameter is obtained from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000. Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin at exact rounding threshold
scenarios.intent: Verify rounding behavior when aggregated margin is already at an exact rounding threshold
given: Aggregated market-risk-component margin before rounding is 46,930,000
Rounding parameter from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: Rounding is applied to the aggregated margin
then: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,930,000
No upward adjustment is required as value is already at rounding threshold
assumptions: Value at exact rounding threshold remains unchanged after rounding

evidence.quote: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000 in the sample Initial Margin Risk Parameter File)
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin calculation validates all required components
scenarios.intent: Verify that aggregated margin calculation validates presence of all required market risk component values
given: Market risk component data is submitted for aggregated margin calculation
when: Aggregated market-risk-component margin calculation is performed
then: Portfolio margin value is present and valid
Flat rate margin value is present and valid
Liquidation risk add-on value is present and valid
Structured product add-on value is present and valid
Corporate action position margin value is present and valid
Holiday add-on value is present and valid
Rounding parameter from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is present and valid
assumptions: All component values are expected to be non-negative numbers
Rounding parameter is expected to be a positive integer

evidence.quote: Calculate aggregated margin derived from market risk components
Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

scenarios.title: Calculate favorable MTM using portfolio values
scenarios.intent: Verify the favorable MTM calculation follows the defined formula for standard portfolio inputs
given: A clearing participant portfolio with Market valuePortfolio of -300,550,000
Contract valuePortfolio of -287,850,000
when: The system calculates favorable MTM using the formula: Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: Favorable MTM equals -12,700,000
The negative number refers to a MTM requirement
Favorable MTM is zero when result is negative
assumptions: Portfolio values are provided in HKD equivalent
Rounding is applied at position level as per Appendix 4.6

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added after applying margin credit

scenarios.title: Calculate net margin when favorable MTM is zero
scenarios.intent: Verify net margin calculation at the boundary where favorable MTM equals zero
given: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin of 46,930,000
Favorable MTM equals zero due to negative MTM requirement result
when: The system calculates net margin using: Maximum(0, Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)
then: Net margin equals Maximum[0, (46,930,000 – 0)]
Net margin equals 46,930,000
assumptions: Boundary condition tests the Maximum function with zero favorable MTM
This represents the case where MTM requirement exists instead of favorable MTM

evidence.quote: Net margin = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)
In such case, favorable MTM is zero

scenarios.title: Validate mutual exclusivity of favorable MTM and MTM requirement
scenarios.intent: Verify that favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive as specified
given: A portfolio with calculated favorable MTM or MTM requirement
when: The system validates the calculation result for reporting
then: Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive
Only one of favorable MTM or MTM requirement has a non-zero value
Absolute value is shown in MTM and Margin Requirement Report
assumptions: Mutual exclusivity is enforced at the data validation level
Report displays absolute value of whichever is applicable

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive
In the MTM and Margin Requirement Report, absolute value of favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) will be shown

scenarios.title: Calculate net margin after applying margin credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the net margin after credit calculation follows the defined formula with standard margin credit
given: A clearing participant with net margin of 46,930,000
Margin credit of 5,000,000 granted to the CP
when: The system calculates net margin after credit using: Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)]
then: Net margin after credit equals Maximum[0, (46,930,000 – 5,000,000)]
Net margin after credit equals 41,930,000
assumptions: Margin credit value of 5,000,000 is the standard amount
Net margin input is the result from Step 2 of section 3.2.5.2

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation
Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Calculate net margin after credit when net margin equals margin credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the boundary condition where net margin equals margin credit resulting in zero
given: A clearing participant with net margin of 5,000,000
Margin credit of 5,000,000 granted to the CP
when: The system calculates net margin after credit using: Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)]
then: Net margin after credit equals Maximum[0, (5,000,000 – 5,000,000)]
Net margin after credit equals 0
assumptions: Boundary tests the Minimum function floor at zero
This represents the threshold where margin credit fully offsets net margin

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]
A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP

scenarios.title: Validate margin credit is granted to each clearing participant
scenarios.intent: Verify that margin credit is correctly assigned and applied for each clearing participant
given: A clearing participant registered in the system
when: The system validates margin credit assignment for the CP
then: Margin credit is granted to each CP
Margin credit value is normally 5,000,000
Margin credit is applied for margin calculation
assumptions: Margin credit is a per-CP configuration value
The normally value of 5,000,000 may be subject to change by HKCC

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP
applied for margin calculation

feature: Total MTM and Margin Requirement Calculation

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement with all components
scenarios.intent: Verify that total MTM and margin requirement is correctly calculated by summing net margin after credit and all other risk components per the documented formula
given: Net margin after credit is 41,930,000
MTM requirement is 12,700,000
Position limit add-on is 487,332
Credit risk add-on is 12,000,000
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000
when: The system calculates total MTM and margin requirement
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals 67,717,332

evidence.quote: Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement when credit risk add-on is not applicable
scenarios.intent: Verify calculation handles the boundary condition when credit risk add-on is zero as explicitly noted for VaR Platform launch
given: Net margin after credit is 41,930,000
MTM requirement is 12,700,000
Position limit add-on is 487,332
Credit risk add-on is 0 (not applicable after VaR Platform launch)
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000
when: The system calculates total MTM and margin requirement
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals 55,717,332
assumptions: Credit risk add-on will be zero after VaR Platform launch as documented in evidence
Other component values remain unchanged from the documented example

evidence.quote: = Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on
Please note that the credit risk add-on will not be applicable upon the launch of VaR Platform

scenarios.title: Reject calculation when required component value is missing
scenarios.intent: Verify that the calculation is rejected when a required input component is missing or null, ensuring data integrity for the margin calculation
given: Net margin after credit is 41,930,000
MTM requirement is missing or null
Position limit add-on is 487,332
Credit risk add-on is 12,000,000
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000
when: The system attempts to calculate total MTM and margin requirement
then: The calculation is rejected with a validation error indicating missing MTM requirement
assumptions: All formula components are required inputs for the calculation to proceed
The system validates input completeness before performing the calculation

evidence.quote: total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin after credit to other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on
